theory.canonical_statement: At critical coupling $\lambda_g > \lambda_c$, the system undergoes topological phase transition characterized by quantized jump in helicity: \begin{equation} \Delta \mathcal{H}_M = n \cdot \Phi_0 \cdot L_\text{link} \end{equation} where $\Phi_0 = h/(2e)$ is the flux quantum and $L_\text{link}$ is the linking number. \section{Experimental Predictions} \subsection{Critical Parameters} \begin{itemize} \item \textbf{Sphere radius}: $R = 10$ cm \item \textbf{Gyro frequency}: $\omega_\text{gyro} = 10^4$ rad/s \item \textbf{Magnetic field}: $B_0 = 1$ T \item \textbf{Material}: High-$\mu_r$ ferrite ($\mu_r \sim 1000$) \end{itemize} \subsection{Predicted Resonances} First five acoustic-magnetic coupled modes: \begin{table}[h] \centering \begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|} \hline Mode $(n,l,m)$ & $f_\text{ac}$ (kHz) & $f_B$ (kHz) & Coupling $\xi$ \\ \hline $(1,1,0)$ & 17.2 & 15.9 & 0.73 \\ $(1,2,0)$ & 28.4 & 26.8 & 0.85 \\ $(2,1,0)$ & 34.5 & 31.8 & 0.92 \\ $(1,3,0)$ & 39.6 & 37.9 & 0.67 \\ $(2,2,0)$ & 56.8 & 53.6 & 0.81 \\ \hline \end{tabular} \caption{Predicted coupled mode frequencies} \end{table} \section{Conclusio
theory.technical_interpretation: Relation: μ_r ∼ 1000 is a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation, written μ_r ∼ 1000. It expresses a scaling or proportionality, capturing how the quantity grows with its parameters. It is built from r. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
